page.title: Assign a role to a team member

second_iteration_design_changes.change: Removed prominent self-access panel from successful state.
second_iteration_design_changes.reason: The page should focus on assigning access to another person, not on displaying the admin's own roles.

second_iteration_design_changes.change: Kept permission check but reduced successful state to compact team-scope copy.
second_iteration_design_changes.reason: Admins need to know the team scope; they do not need a diagnostic display of their own roles when authorised.

second_iteration_design_changes.change: Set the email input to two-thirds width and moved user ID into a details component with a half-width input.
second_iteration_design_changes.reason: The visual affordance should match the expected content length and keep the normal path focused on human-readable email.

second_iteration_design_changes.change: Replaced the role select with radios.
second_iteration_design_changes.reason: The roles are few, consequential and need visible descriptions at the point of decision.

second_iteration_design_changes.change: Replaced arbitrary datetime input with governed duration radios and conditional day/month/year date input.
second_iteration_design_changes.reason: Role expiry is a governed access-period decision, not a timestamp-entry task.
The write action should not occur on ordinary form submit for a sensitive administrative task.

client_behaviour.write_flow: form submit validates and renders review; confirm button performs POST

checks: removal of self-access panel from successful state
stylesheet hooks for width affordance and review state

unit_test_failure.message: Expected visual walkthrough registry to include public route: /pages/team/role-assignments/index.html
unit_test_failure.cause: The new public HTML route was not registered in visual-walkthrough.config.mjs.
fix: Added deterministic Team Admin /api/me fixture data.
Configured the default walkthrough state to use the deterministic Team Admin context.

feature_preview_failure.reported_message: Could not confirm your team role access. Load failed
feature_preview_failure.cause: The client forced Pages preview origins to call the Worker origin directly instead of using the Pages same-origin /api/* redirect proxy.
fix: Changed the default API base to the same-origin empty string.
Kept explicit override support through document.documentElement.dataset.apiOrigin and window.API_ORIGIN.
Updated route-state tests to assert the Worker URL is not hard-coded.

cloudflare_access_runtime_evidence.source: manual branch-preview retest reported by user
configuration_learning: The original AUD value was a Pages web analytics tag, not an Access AUD.
CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN must not include https:// for the current Worker code path.
The Worker receives the Pages Access JWT through the Pages proxy path, so the Worker AUD value must align with the Pages Access application AUD for that route.

boundaries: No user search endpoint is created.
No users are created by this UI.
No team memberships are created by this UI.
The target user is not resolved before the check-and-confirm panel in this slice.
No full team member list is added.
The D1 route-status workflow is not run by this PR.
next_steps_after_merge: Open /pages/team/role-assignments/ with the seeded Team Admin account.
Confirm the page shows active team scope.
Confirm the page hides the form for users without role.assign.
Choose a non-sensitive role and governed duration.
Confirm the check-and-confirm panel appears before the write.
Assign a non-sensitive role to an existing active team member.
